title: Latvian foreign minister confirms consulate in Ukraine was hit by deadly Russian attack
content: Russian strikes have damaged Latvia's Honorary Consulate in eastern Ukraine, killing six civilians and wounding dozens. EU and Baltic leaders condemned the attack as a deliberate strike on civilians and diplomatic infrastructure.
snippet: Russian strikes have damaged Latvia's Honorary Consulate in eastern Ukraine, killing six civilians and wounding dozens. EU and Baltic leaders condemned the attack as a deliberate strike on civilians

title: Poland's opposition PiS splits ahead of crucial 2027 elections
content: PiS leader Jarosław Kaczyński announced that more than 30 MPs had left the party following a bitter power struggle with former Prime Minister Mateusz Morawiecki. It marks the biggest split in Poland's main opposition party in more than two decades.
snippet: PiS leader Jarosław Kaczyński announced that more than 30 MPs had left the party following a bitter power struggle with former Prime Minister Mateusz Morawiecki. It marks the biggest split in Poland's

title: EU seeks dialogue with US as tensions rise after Google fine
content: Despite the trade deal struck between Brussels and Washington, relations remain volatile, with US lawmakers pushing to launch a trade investigation into the EU's tech rules.
snippet: Despite the trade deal struck between Brussels and Washington, relations remain volatile, with US lawmakers pushing to launch a trade investigation into the EU's tech rules.

title: Estonia's girls only HK Unicorn Squad is helping build the country's next tech generation
content:
"Is everyone ready for camp?"

Hundreds of young girls cheered back with excitement as Taavi Kotka, smiling from the stage, opened a unique summer camp held at the Viimsi School outside Tallinn.
snippet: "Is everyone ready for camp?" Hundreds of young girls cheered back with excitement as Taavi Kotka, smiling from the stage, opened a unique summer camp held at the Viimsi School outside Tallinn.

title: EU far right cries foul, but Commission unfazed by Magyar's 'Purgatorium'
content: Orbán allies across Europe have raised concerns over Hungarian democracy after the president's mandate was ended and Fidesz's headquarters raided as part of a corruption probe, but the European Commission appears broadly aligned with a push to dismantle Orbán's political operation.
snippet: Orbán allies across Europe have raised concerns over Hungarian democracy after the president's mandate was ended and Fidesz's headquarters raided as part of a corruption probe, but the European

title: Google fine 'creates uncertainty' for EU-US trade deal, US Trade Representative says
content: Washington says the EU's €890m fine against Google under the Digital Markets Act jeopardises the EU-US trade deal, calling it "massive uncertainty" for US exporters.
snippet: Washington says the EU's €890m fine against Google under the Digital Markets Act jeopardises the EU-US trade deal, calling it "massive uncertainty" for US exporters.

title: Man jailed for life over terror plot targeting UK military academy
content: Bashir left the UK two days after the synagogue attack and was arrested at Manchester Airport in November 2025 when he returned from a family wedding in Pakistan.
snippet: Bashir left the UK two days after the synagogue attack and was arrested at Manchester Airport in November 2025 when he returned from a family wedding in Pakistan.

title: EU temporarily extends controversial chat-scanning regime until 2028
content: EU countries have adopted a temporary regime allowing messaging services to voluntarily detect child sexual abuse material until 2028. End-to-end encrypted apps like WhatsApp and Signal remain exempt.
snippet: EU countries have adopted a temporary regime allowing messaging services to voluntarily detect child sexual abuse material until 2028. End-to-end encrypted apps like WhatsApp and Signal remain exempt.

title: 'No real sense of direction': EU struggling to find path to sanctioning Israeli settlements
content: EU ambassadors have failed to agree on the next steps for sanctioning Israeli settlements – and with no majority in favour of any option, diplomats have admitted there's no clear way forward.
snippet: EU ambassadors have failed to agree on the next steps for sanctioning Israeli settlements – and with no majority in favour of any option, diplomats have admitted there's no clear way forward.

title: One dead and four injured after Czech military helicopter crashes at air base
content: An investigation into the incident is underway. All of the military's H-1 helicopters have been grounded until it is completed, the Czech armed forces said in a statement.
snippet: An investigation into the incident is underway. All of the military's H-1 helicopters have been grounded until it is completed, the Czech armed forces said in a statement.

title: Guiding Europe? Ireland's six-month EU Presidency takes centre stage on The Ring
content: On this week's episode of The Ring, Euronews' weekly debate show, we travel to Ireland, which has just taken over the rotating presidency of the Council of the European Union, to hear what issues are keeping politicians up at night.

title: Germany's Europe minister on EU budget: 'Options are limited'
content: For leading German politician Gunther Krichbaum (CDU), Minister of State for Europe, it is time for the EU to rethink and seek new paths, on enlargement and the budget alike.
snippet: For leading German politician Gunther Krichbaum (CDU), Minister of State for Europe, it is time for the EU to rethink and seek new paths, on enlargement and the budget alike.

title: Euronews in Cotignac: How a historic French village survived a night of wildfire
content: Euronews reports on the ground in France, where a blaze tore through a medieval village in Provence overnight, destroying dozens of homes and burning thousands of hectares, as residents opened their doors to strangers.

title: Could Gianni Infantino really become UN Secretary General if Trump puts him forward?
content: US President can push FIFA’s boss to lead the United Nations, but not impose him over the other candidates – and the other permanent members of the Security Council would need to endorse him.
snippet: US President can push FIFA’s boss to lead the United Nations, but not impose him over the other candidates – and the other permanent members of the Security Council would need to endorse him.

title: Former UK water company CEO charged over alleged water test fraud
content: Under UK law, intentionally altering wastewater flows during regulatory sampling is a criminal offence. If convicted, Wright faces a maximum sentence of 10 years in prison.
snippet: Under UK law, intentionally altering wastewater flows during regulatory sampling is a criminal offence. If convicted, Wright faces a maximum sentence of 10 years in prison.

title: Zelenskyy replaces Oleksandr Syrskyi with Mykhailo Drapatyi as Ukraine's commander-in-chief
content: Under pressure from civil society and widespread protests across Ukraine, President Zelenskyy on Tuesday replaced General Syrskyi with Mykhailo Drapatyi as Ukraine's commander-in-chief.
snippet: Under pressure from civil society and widespread protests across Ukraine, President Zelenskyy on Tuesday replaced General Syrskyi with Mykhailo Drapatyi as Ukraine's commander-in-chief.

title: Spanish singer Julio Iglesias sues Spain's deputy PM Yolanda Díaz, demands €50,000
content: The singer has filed a lawsuit in the Supreme Court against the deputy prime minister, alleging a violation of his right to honour and seeking €50,000 over remarks she made about sexual abuse allegations brought by two former employees.
snippet: The singer has filed a lawsuit in the Supreme Court against the deputy prime minister, alleging a violation of his right to honour and seeking €50,000 over remarks she made about sexual abuse

title: France becomes the first country in Europe to officially ban social media for under-15s
content: Other EU countries such as Greece, Slovenia and Sweden are planning to restrict minors' access to social media.
snippet: Other EU countries such as Greece, Slovenia and Sweden are planning to restrict minors' access to social media.

title: Brussels prepares first digital rulebook fine against Google, risking Trump's ire: media reports
content: German media reports say the EU executive is set to announce the fine against the tech titan on Thursday in a move that could escalate tensions with the Trump administration over digital services.
snippet: German media reports say the EU executive is set to announce the fine against the tech titan on Thursday in a move that could escalate tensions with the Trump administration over digital services.

title: UK to remove 5% tax on electricity bills as Burnham holds first cabinet meeting
content: The UK's new prime minister, Andy Burnham, has held his first cabinet meeting, telling ministers he will set out his plans "over the coming days" and promised to reveal how his government will pay for any reductions in tax.

title: Irish police foil suspected dissident republican car bomb near Northern Ireland border
content: Irish police have arrested a man and a woman after intercepting what they described as a "highly sophisticated" car bomb believed to be linked to dissident republican groups.
snippet: Irish police have arrested a man and a woman after intercepting what they described as a "highly sophisticated" car bomb believed to be linked to dissident republican groups.

title: Dutch legend van Bommel appointed new Belgium head coach
content: "Together with my coaching staff, we want to build a team that is disciplined, ambitious and brave enough to compete with the very best," van Bommel said in a statement.
snippet: "Together with my coaching staff, we want to build a team that is disciplined, ambitious and brave enough to compete with the very best," van Bommel said in a statement.

title: Fact check: Claims resurface that ex-UK PM Boris Johnson sabotaged Ukraine peace deal for bribe
content: In an X post from 21 July, Kremlin envoy Kirill Dimitriev alleged that the UK's former Prime Minister Boris Johnson "sabotaged" peace efforts between Russia and Ukraine in exchange for money, but facts say otherwise.

title: Podcast | Brussels eyes political reshuffles in London and Budapest
content: In a week of political upheaval in Europe, Hungary got rid of its president and the UK's seventh prime minister in a decade entered office. What does this mean for Brussels?
snippet: In a week of political upheaval in Europe, Hungary got rid of its president and the UK's seventh prime minister in a decade entered office. What does this mean for Brussels?

title: German rail network to ban alcohol from 5,400 train stations by October, with some exemptions
content: Germany's national railway operator Deutsche Bahn will introduce a ban on alcohol at all 5,400 stations by 15 October. There will be exemptions, however, including for station bars and restaurants.
snippet: Germany's national railway operator Deutsche Bahn will introduce a ban on alcohol at all 5,400 stations by 15 October. There will be exemptions, however, including for station bars and restaurants.

title: From housing to healthcare: What are Europeans worried about in 2026?
content: Is the economy, housing, immigration or healthcare the biggest concern facing your country? It depends on where in Europe you live.
snippet: Is the economy, housing, immigration or healthcare the biggest concern facing your country? It depends on where in Europe you live.

title: Romania says F-16 shot down drone over the country's airspace
content: Romania's Ministry of Defence said the jet engaged a drone with an air-to-air missile while carrying out an air policing mission.
snippet: Romania's Ministry of Defence said the jet engaged a drone with an air-to-air missile while carrying out an air policing mission.

title: German Chancellor Friedrich Merz announces cabinet overhaul
content: Nina Warken is set to succeed Thorsten Frei (CDU) as head of the chancellery. Carsten Linnemann, who had been serving as the general secretary of Merz's CDU party, is then to take over Warken's role as health minister.
snippet: Nina Warken is set to succeed Thorsten Frei (CDU) as head of the chancellery. Carsten Linnemann, who had been serving as the general secretary of Merz's CDU party, is then to take over Warken's role

title: Ireland finds 'no evidence' to conclude alumina sales prop up Russia's war
content: The long-awaited investigation into Ireland's alumina exports to Russia has concluded on an ambivalent note, with the government neither fully confirming nor denying any link to Vladimir Putin's war machine.

title: Southern Europe still battling wildfires as Spain declares national emergency in Madrid region
content: Spain has declared a national emergency as wildfires threaten the Madrid region, while France seeks EU help and Italy battles blazes across Sicily.
snippet: Spain has declared a national emergency as wildfires threaten the Madrid region, while France seeks EU help and Italy battles blazes across Sicily.

title: 'Expect more action from the US after the Google fine,' top EU lawmaker says
content: German Socialist MEP Bernd Lange, the European Parliament's trade chief, told Euronews that he hopes the new US tariffs will be challenged in US courts and said that targeting the EU over forced labour was "crazy" given its stringent regulations.

title: EU’s €1bn-a-day trade gap with China an 'unsustainable reality', says EU Parliament VP
content: Javi López, European Parliament vice-president, says the European Union's trade imbalance with China "cannot continue", with the bloc running a €1bn-a-day trade deficit.
snippet: Javi López, European Parliament vice-president, says the European Union's trade imbalance with China "cannot continue", with the bloc running a €1bn-a-day trade deficit.

title: Europe Today: Trump imposes new tariffs as EU agrees fresh sanctions on Russia
content: US President Donald Trump is imposing a new wave of tariffs on trading partners, including the EU, with levies ranging between 10 and 12.5%. Bernd Lange, the EU Parliament top lawmaker on trade, reacts to this measure. Also in Brussels: The EU has agreed a new round of sanctions against Russia.

title: Safeguards for new EU members need objective criteria, Montenegro's ambassador says
content: Montenegro is backing new EU rule-of-law safeguards for future members while insisting they must be based on objective criteria and not create a second tier of membership.
snippet: Montenegro is backing new EU rule-of-law safeguards for future members while insisting they must be based on objective criteria and not create a second tier of membership.

title: Chaotic sanctions negotiations expose cracks in EU front versus Russia
content: New round of EU sanctions against Russia shaped by a series of veto threats as member states sought to protect national interests. The final package, significantly watered down, has exposed growing cracks within the bloc.

title: Fact check: No evidence links Norway fire to alleged Israeli settlement import ban
content: A massive fire in Norway has destroyed more than 100 homes and forced hundreds of people to evacuate. As the blaze spread, posts on social media began linking it to claims that Oslo had banned imports of Israeli settlement products. The Cube investigates.

title: Lavrov tells Rubio 'unacceptable' for US to continue arms sales to Kyiv

snippet: Lavrov and Rubio are also said to have discussed the situation in the Persian Gulf. Washington's focus has largely shifted to the region, where it remains locked in conflict with Iran.

title: Brussels slaps €890 million fine on Google despite looming new US tariffs
content: Brussels fined Google €890 million under its Big Tech rulebook for favouring its own services in search results and restricting app developers, despite Washington preparing a fresh set of trade tariffs as current ones are expiring.

title: Far-right activists to sue European Commission over 'remigration' rejection
content: The European Commission has rejected a European Citizens' Initiative seeking to suspend asylum and family reunification and return "unintegrated" non-European migrants to their countries of origin. Its organisers are preparing a legal challenge.
snippet: The European Commission has rejected a European Citizens' Initiative seeking to suspend asylum and family reunification and return "unintegrated" non-European migrants to their countries of origin.

title: "NATO will defend Bulgaria against any action by Iran," official warns
content: Iran has threatened Bulgaria after the government sit aid plans to host US military aircraft. Tehran warned Sofia must not allow its territory to become a launchpad for operations against Tehran. Colonel Martin O'Donnell tells Euronews NATO is ready to defend Bulgaria should it come under attack.
snippet: Iran has threatened Bulgaria after the government sit aid plans to host US military aircraft. Tehran warned Sofia must not allow its territory to become a launchpad for operations against Tehran.

title: Putin stepping up hybrid war on Latvia because he’s ‘losing in Ukraine’, PM Kulbergs says
content: In an exclusive interview with Europe Today, Latvia’s Prime Minister Andris Kulbergs says the “top level of the Russian mob” is behind a recent uptick in attempted migrant crossings into his country from Belarus.
snippet: In an exclusive interview with Europe Today, Latvia’s Prime Minister Andris Kulbergs says the “top level of the Russian mob” is behind a recent uptick in attempted migrant crossings into his country

title: EU agrees new sanctions against Russia as Greece secures LNG exemption
content: The deal reached by ambassadors grants Greece an exemption to continue shipping Russian LNG to non-EU clients for the foreseeable future.
snippet: The deal reached by ambassadors grants Greece an exemption to continue shipping Russian LNG to non-EU clients for the foreseeable future.

title: Green homes: Which countries are making their buildings more energy efficient?
content: Energy efficiency levels are rising, but not quickly enough, according to Brussels, which has launched an infringement procedure against all EU member states.

content: Two firefighters died in France, and another died in Italy while working to tackle wildfires that have devastated Europe this week.

title: Air passenger rights: This is how you get your money back
content: The European Council approved the agreement on stronger air passenger rights on 13 July, following MEPs’ backing on 7 July. The new rules come as passengers struggle to fully exercise their rights, leaving airlines with €3.2 billion in unpaid compensation.

title: Europe Today: Latvian PM Andris Kulbergs speaks exclusively to Euronews as Rubio meets Lavrov
content: Andris Kulbergs became Latvia's Prime Minister in May after the previous coalition government collapsed over its handling of stray drones. He speaks exclusively to Euronews as US Secretary of State, Marco Rubio, meets today Russian Foreign Minister Sergey Lavrov to discuss ending the war in Ukraine.

title: Hungary's prosecutor resigns under pressure in political win for Magyar
content: Hungary's Prosecutor General, Gábor Bálint Nagy, has resigned amid mounting political pressure as Prime Minister Péter Magyar seeks to replace Orbán-era officials. The move follows allegations of political bias by lawyers for detained Ukrainian cash couriers in a cross-border cash seizure case.

content: Video footage circulating on social media shows the moment the two trams collided. One tram can be seen hurtling into another stationary railcar.
snippet: Video footage circulating on social media shows the moment the two trams collided. One tram can be seen hurtling into another stationary railcar.

title: French environment minister convinced to stay on after announcing resignation
content: Barbut had resigned earlier on Wednesday and criticized the government in a LinkedIn post, saying it had reneged on promises over an agricultural bill to reintroduce certain pesticides.
snippet: Barbut had resigned earlier on Wednesday and criticized the government in a LinkedIn post, saying it had reneged on promises over an agricultural bill to reintroduce certain pesticides.

title: EU's food safety agency reduces safe daily intake level of 'forever chemical' TFA
content: TFAS are a type of perfluoroalkyl and polyfluoroalkyl substance (PFAS), often known as "forever chemicals" as they are long-lasting in the environment.

title: EU set to bow to fresh US tariffs after current regime lapses
content: Despite considering the new duties "unjustified," the Commission said its priority was to ensure tariffs on EU goods remain below the 15% cap agreed under the EU-US trade deal in July 2025.
snippet: Despite considering the new duties "unjustified," the Commission said its priority was to ensure tariffs on EU goods remain below the 15% cap agreed under the EU-US trade deal in July 2025.
